title: Patriots free agency live updates: Tracking New England’s top offseason pickups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots have over $50 million in cap space as NFL free agency begins, aiming to improve their roster.
Major roster changes include releasing Stefon Diggs and trading away center Garrett Bradbury for cap flexibility.
Key free agent targets for Patriots: edge rushers like Boye Mafe, and potential receivers including Alec Pierce.

title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, 46, is running the Boston Marathon as part of her goal to complete marathons in all 50 states.
Harding has completed over a dozen marathons since her colorectal cancer diagnosis and subsequent recovery in 2021.
She is raising $12,000 for Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge Team, benefiting cancer research, with 10 states remaining after Boston.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Federal judge Richard Stearns dismissed the lawsuit of Babson College student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza, deported to Honduras in 2025.
Lopez Belloza's lawyer claimed ICE used misleading tactics, but the judge found no intent to conceal her location from her counsel.
Despite the deportation error, Lopez Belloza continues her Babson studies remotely from Honduras; legal team has filed an appeal.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson praises Derrick White as a 'top-5 player' in the NBA using advanced analytics.
Derrick White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists despite a decline in shooting percentage this season.
With Jayson Tatum's return, White resumes his versatile role, contributing defensively and impacting games for the Celtics.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 109-98, with Jaylen Brown scoring 23 points and contributing significantly in the second half.
Jayson Tatum returned to action from injury, scoring 20 points in 27 minutes despite not yet being fully healthy.
Derrick White's performance was praised, with him noted as a key 'connector' for the team, contributing both defensively and offensively.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against a $30 million annual contract for Colts WR Alec Pierce.
Alec Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, with 47 catches for 1,003 yards last season.
New England Patriots have over $50 million in cap space after releasing Stefon Diggs, but need multiple upgrades.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide on March 4, according to the local police department.
Dougal graduated from Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December, joining North Kingstown department shortly thereafter.
Family requests donations to North Kingstown Animal Shelter and Tunnel to Towers Foundation in Dougal's memory.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots considering unheralded free agents, including Vikings WR Jalen Nailor with 10 touchdowns in two seasons.
Giants TE Daniel Bellinger emerges as a potential complementary option with 19 receptions and two touchdowns last season.
Bengals' EDGE Joseph Ossai tallied 10 sacks and 89 tackles over last two seasons, intriguing Patriots as possible signee.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, minority owner of the Raiders, may have influenced the trade decision to keep Maxx Crosby from the Patriots.
The Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall pick in the 2026 draft.
The Patriots, seeking to upgrade at edge rusher, are exploring options in free agency including Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce is expected to earn at least $27 million annually if he enters free agency.
The Indianapolis Colts are negotiating with Alec Pierce after not placing a franchise tag on him.
The New England Patriots are reportedly interested in wide receiver Alec Pierce for their team.
